name: Reward Fairness Guard
purpose: Defines a report-only guard for reviewing internal points, XP, streaks, mission completion, challenge rewards, anti-cheat, beginner/advanced fairness, and no-pay-to-win boundaries before WellFit reward concepts move toward runtime implementation.

connectedSources.usage: Inherit preview-only Mission/Buddy/Economy authority boundaries and server-ledger requirements.
Classify reward, mission completion, anti-cheat, token/payment, protected-data, and authority changes as high or critical until explicit review approves scoped implementation.
Align internal points, XP, streaks, and beta economy language with internal-only, non-money-near guardrails.
Align reward and completion findings with server-authoritative completion and Firestore hardening requirements.

reviewAreas.label: Interne Punkte
checks: Points remain internal beta mechanics and do not become tokens, cash, payout balance, or final ledger writes from the client.
Point earning and spending concepts remain server-authoritative or clearly preview-only until backend authority is approved.
Point totals do not grant unfair completion authority, anti-cheat bypass, or paid advantage.

reviewAreas.label: XP
checks: XP recognizes effort, learning, consistency, and safe recovery without medical or guaranteed outcome claims.
XP changes are not final-authorized by the frontend, Buddy, AR client, or local-only code.
XP curves do not force excessive activity, sleep loss, shame loops, or unfair beginner exclusion.

reviewAreas.label: Streaks
checks: Streaks support recovery, pauses, illness, travel, and lower-energy days without punishment-for-rest mechanics.
Streak repair or protection cannot be purchased in a way that creates pay-to-win progress.
Streak language avoids shame, guilt, manipulation, and dark-pattern pressure.

reviewAreas.label: Mission Completion
checks: Mission completion remains evidence submission, preview, or server-authoritative finalization only.
Client, Buddy, AR, and UI flows must not final-authorize mission completion.
Protected data such as health, location, camera, biometric, or child/minor signals cannot be direct completion authority without explicit reviewed design.

reviewAreas.label: Challenge Rewards
checks: Challenge rewards remain fair, transparent, internal, non-cash, non-token, and non-betting by default.
Leaderboards and PvP/challenge concepts must avoid humiliation, coercive comparison, wagering, payouts, and overtraining pressure.
Reward distribution accounts for accessibility, novice paths, recovery, team balance, and anti-farming limits.

reviewAreas.label: Anti-Cheat
checks: Anti-cheat can produce report-only flags in this guard, not enforcement decisions or final denials.
Anti-cheat design requires server-side authority, explainability, appeal/review planning, and protected-data minimization.
Anti-cheat cannot rely solely on client-controlled values, paid status, or protected data.

reviewAreas.label: Fairness fuer Anfaenger/Fortgeschrittene
checks: Beginners receive achievable paths, safe alternatives, onboarding-friendly XP/points, and protection from social humiliation.
Advanced users can pursue mastery without making beginner progress irrelevant or unsafe.
Difficulty, rewards, streaks, and challenges should account for accessibility, recovery, age-appropriate design, and context without runtime profiling by this guard.

reviewAreas.label: Keine Pay-to-win Mechanik
checks: Payment, purchases, subscriptions, sponsors, tokens, NFTs, wallets, payouts, and marketplace features must not buy mission completion, challenge wins, anti-cheat bypass, XP superiority, or reward authority.
Paid or sponsored concepts require fair non-purchase alternatives and explicit human review before implementation.
No scarcity timers, paid streak rescue loops, lootbox-like rewards, betting, wagering, or money-near advantage mechanics are allowed by this guard.

nextSafeMaintenanceTask: Keep the reward fairness guard synchronized with mission/buddy/economy, risk, internal economy, and server completion guardrails before any reward runtime work proceeds.
